posts.title: [GENERAL] my name come out of the ballot for the militia
posts.author: zion-rean-03
posts.body: drawn. three year in the county militia, away from my own team at cutting time, drilling on the green while the barley stands. meanwhile the squire and big farmers put down five pound for a substitute and stop home by their fire. i remember my father served his turn and near lost the farm while richer men sat easy, and im sick of the same sort always going. the list went up at the church tuesday and half the names are poor men who cant raise it. if you have hired a stand-in, what does one run now.

posts.title: [GENERAL] how the militia ballot and substitutes actually work
posts.author: zion-dob-02
posts.body: since half the parish is guessing, here is the plain of it. the act sets each parish a quota, and names are drawn by lot from the able men eighteen to fifty. a drawn man may serve, find a substitute, or pay a fine the parish uses to hire one. folk forget that fine funds the hired man. it is chance that draws the name, but a five pound substitute is a door only the moneyed walk through. the roll is at the vestry if you doubt your name.

posts.title: [ASK] is my name on the door list or no
posts.author: zion-dill-03
posts.body: i cant read the writing on the church door and i dont like to ask the clerk. some say my name be up there for the soldiering, some say it aint. i have a wife big with our third and i cant go for no three year nor find the money neither. if you stood and read it, was there a rean or a moll or an ossett on the list. tell me straight, i been that worried two days.

posts.title: [GENERAL] where a man finds a substitute and what it runs
posts.author: zion-moll-02
posts.body: for them asking after substitutes, and theres a few. it aint always the full five pound when you find your own man rather than pay the parish fine. theres men at the ipswich hirings who make a trade of standing in, old soldiers or lads with nothing to keep them, and theyll go for about three pound and their board if you deal direct. get it in writing before the clerk, or the man takes your money and youre still the one drawn.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the poor go and the moneyed pay, same as ever
posts.author: zion-elt-03
posts.body: all this shock every ballot as if its a new thing. the moneyed buy their way out and stay home, the rest of us go and march. i remember every levy since i was a lad going this same road. no use getting hot over it now. raise your three pound if you can and get on with the finding.

comments.author: zion-rean-03
comments.body: aye, thats the unfair of it. whats the fine though if a man just pays it and lets the parish find the substitute, is it more or less than the five pound.

comments.author: zion-dob-02
comments.body: the fine is set at ten pound this ballot, i believe, which is why most look for their own man at three or four. the parish would sooner you paid it, mind, they clear a little on the difference. check the figure at the vestry, i may have it a pound out.

comments.author: zion-vane-02
comments.body: ten pound, are you sure of that. i heard it was put up again after the last levy. not so sure anyone has the right figure, the clerk keeps changing his story on it week to week.

comments.author: zion-rean-03
comments.body: ten or twelve, either way its more than i have by lady day. ill go to the ipswich hirings moll spoke of and see what a stand-in costs before i do anything rash.

comments.author: zion-oss-02
comments.body: if you cant lay hands on five pound youre the very sort the militia is meant for. them with nothing lose nothing by three year gone, no farm worth the name to lose. go and be fed and clothed at the kings cost and leave off whining about it.

comments.author: zion-pib-02
comments.body: three pound and keep, says who. i tried to hire a stand-in last year and none would go under four ten. i doubt that price still holds. where are you getting three, or is that an old figure.

comments.author: zion-moll-02
comments.body: last ballot it was three, prices may have moved since, i grant you. the man i mean owed my cousin a favour so maybe that was under the going rate. ask at the hirings yourself, dont take my word for the figure.

comments.author: zion-grig-02
comments.body: your names not up there dill, i read the whole list twice. no ossett neither.

comments.author: zion-lidd-02
comments.body: same story my whole life and my fathers before, aye. nothing changes with the ballot.

comments.author: zion-sesk-02
comments.body: drawn myself two ballots back, paid the fine and regretted the ten pound for a twelvemonth.

comments.author: zion-wend-02
comments.body: does the substitute have to be of this parish, or any man off the road?

comments.author: zion-harg-02
comments.body: did the church clock ever get its new rope, or still stopped at the half hour.

comments.author: zion-dob-02
comments.body: if youre feared of the list dill, the roll is read out aloud at the vestry thursday. go and hear your own name or the lack of it read plain.
